steps.title: Identify the logical relationship the transition must signal
steps.content: The previous sentence states a broad claim: "a new, more diverse generation of artists is stepping up to the podium." The sentence with the blank then names two specific conductors — Alondra de la Parra and Lina Gonzalez-Granados — who did exactly that. The transition must signal that these conductors are examples proving the claim, not merely additional facts, a final item, or a concession.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘in addition,’: "In addition" simply piles on another item; it does not mark the conductors as illustrative examples of the claim. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘lastly,’: "Lastly" implies a final item in a sequence that was already being listed; the passage gives no prior list, so this creates a false structure. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘granted,’: "Granted" signals a concession or exception to the preceding statement, the opposite of what is needed here. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘for instance,’: "For instance" introduces specific examples that support a general claim — precisely the function these two conductors serve. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
